target.title: fix(gateway): accept --port on gateway health and probe (#79100)
target.body:
## Summary

`gateway run --port 18789` accepts `--port`, but the adjacent query subcommands rejected the same flag:

- `openclaw gateway health --port 18789` → `error: unknown option '--port'`
- `openclaw gateway probe --port 18789` → `error: unknown option '--port'`

So scripts that start a gateway on a controlled local port could not target it through `health`/`probe` with the same visible flag shape as `run`, and the `--url` workaround disables config credential fallback (forcing explicit `--token`/`--password`).

### Fix

Accept `--port` on the gateway query commands in **both spellings** — before the subcommand (`gateway --port 18789 health`, inherited from the parent) **and after it** (`gateway health --port 18789`, the exact spelling the issue reports) — by patching `config.gateway.port` rather than synthesizing a `ws://` URL, so configured local auth and the TLS scheme (`ws://` vs `wss://`) are preserved:

- `gatewayCallOpts` gains a `--port` option, so `health`, `call`, `usage-cost`, and `stability` accept `--port` after the subcommand; `resolveGatewayRpcOptions` still inherits the parent `--port` for the before-subcommand spelling.
- `callGatewayCli` loads the real config and overrides only `gateway.port`, so configured credentials still apply (no forced `--token`/`--password`).
- `gateway probe` gains its own `--port` and passes a **numeric** port to `gatewayStatusCommand`, which patches `config.gateway.port` before `resolveTargets` derives the scheme.
- Invalid ports are rejected via the shared strict `parsePort`.

(Per the review feedback, the earlier direct `CHANGELOG.md` edit was dropped.)

Fixes #79100

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: `gateway health` and `gateway probe` rejected the `--port` that `gateway run` accepts — now both the issue's after-subcommand spelling (`gateway health --port N`) and the before-subcommand spelling (`gateway --port N health`) resolve the port by patching `config.gateway.port` (preserving local auth/TLS) instead of erroring or ignoring it.

**Real environment tested**: vitest (`src/cli/gateway-cli.coverage.test.ts`) driving the **real** `registerGatewayCli` Commander program through `runGatewayCommand([...])` argv parsing — the actual option-resolution path this PR changes — with `callGateway`/`gatewayStatusCommand` stubbed only at the RPC boundary to capture the resolved options. The full `openclaw` CLI binary requires a monorepo `pnpm build` (the GitHub source tree ships no `dist/`); the socket/connect layer below option resolution is unchanged by this PR.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/gateway-cli.coverage.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**: `Test Files  1 passed (1)` / `Tests  23 passed (23)`, including four #79100 cases — `health` and `probe` × before-subcommand and after-subcommand spellings. Reverting only the source edits while keeping the tests flips the new cases to failing, proving the tests bind the fix.

**Observed result after fix**: For all four invocations the port reaches the RPC/probe layer as a config patch, not a URL override — `gateway [--port 18799] health [--port 18799]` calls `callGateway` once with `config.gateway.port === 18799` and `url === undefined`; `gateway [--port 18799] probe [--port 18799]` calls `gatewayStatusCommand` once with numeric `port === 18799` and no string `url`, so `resolveTargets` derives `ws://` vs `wss://` from the loaded config's TLS settings rather than a hard-coded plaintext URL.

**What was not tested**: A live gateway socket round-trip on a real non-default port — the GitHub source tree is unbuilt and a full CLI build was not available in this environment, so the change is verified by driving the real Commander program and asserting the resolved RPC/probe options rather than connecting a live gateway. A maintainer can confirm end-to-end locally with `openclaw gateway run --port 18799 --dev --auth none --bind loopback` followed by `openclaw gateway health --port 18799` and `openclaw gateway probe --port 18799`; the connect path itself is unchanged.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: alix-007
